signals.name: Demand spike goes unabsorbed
signals.description: A sudden rush or wave of covers hits the venue faster than the operation can absorb without visible service strain.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: demand-capacity mismatch expressed through restaurant rush absorption
evidence_types: queue expands immediately after demand spike
service strain appears within minutes of surge

signals.name: Last-minute volume shift hits the floor
signals.description: Reservations, walk-ins, large parties, or timing changes bunch together late enough to disrupt pacing and reset the guest flow.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: last-minute volume shift expressed through restaurant arrival flow
evidence_types: late group timing reshapes floor pacing

signals.name: Skill mix is mismatched to the work
signals.description: The people present are not the right combination of capability and confidence for the pressure or complexity of the shift.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: capability mismatch expressed through restaurant shift deployment
evidence_types: experienced coverage too thin for the shift
complex station left to underprepared staff

signals.name: Critical role is left uncovered
signals.description: A role that protects flow or standards is missing entirely, forcing the rest of the venue to improvise around the gap.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: uncovered critical role expressed through restaurant service flow
evidence_types: no expo or floor lead in a pressured window
missing host, runner, or section owner

signals.name: Shift or day starts unprepared
signals.description: The venue begins service without the minimum coordination, setup, and readiness needed to absorb normal pressure.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: shift starts unprepared expressed through restaurant readiness failures
evidence_types: team enters the shift chasing basics

signals.name: Rework or repeat work is observed
signals.description: The team is doing work twice, correcting preventable misses, or remaking steps that should have landed cleanly the first time.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: rework expressed through restaurant service and production execution
evidence_types: service steps repeated because first pass failed

signals.name: Work pace breaks under load
signals.description: The venue can move acceptably at low pressure, but once load rises the pace becomes visibly unstable and backlog forms quickly.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: work pace breaks under load expressed through restaurant floor execution

signals.name: Same mistake repeats across people
signals.description: The same operational miss shows up across different team members, suggesting the issue lives in the system rather than in one person.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: repeated mistakes across people expressed through restaurant training and learning failure
evidence_types: error pattern travels across the team
different people repeat the same avoidable failure

signals.name: Training is not translating into execution
signals.description: The team has been shown the standard, but the expected behavior still does not appear reliably in live service.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: training not translating to execution expressed through restaurant standards drift
evidence_types: trained behavior missing in live shift
coaching happened but standards still do not land

signals.name: Follow-through is missing
signals.description: The venue discusses the right action, but the reset, check-back, or accountability loop does not actually land afterward.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: missing follow-through expressed through restaurant management rhythm

signals.name: Tool or system is unavailable when needed
signals.description: A device, system, or basic tool the venue relies on is missing, offline, dead, or otherwise unavailable during live work.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: tool or system unavailable expressed through restaurant live service friction
evidence_types: service tool missing at moment of use
system outage or device dead during shift

signal_failure_map.source_ref: unabsorbed restaurant demand spikes are the clearest field signal of deployment mismatch
weak live leadership often leaves a rush spike unabsorbed instead of actively reshaped
late arrival shifts usually surface first as broken host pacing and quote control
compressed arrival waves also expose weak redeployment capacity in real time
the wrong capability mix on shift is often a live training-depth problem, not only a headcount problem
mismatched capability distribution is also a deployment-design failure
an uncovered critical role is a direct deployment failure in restaurant operations
when nobody covers a critical role, ownership boundaries are usually weak as well
a day that starts unprepared is a direct restaurant readiness signal
restaurant day-start drift often travels with no real briefing reset
rework usually means standard execution is not landing cleanly
repeat work also signals that steps and handoffs are not designed clearly enough
pace collapse under pressure is often the live signature of poor deployment fit
if pace collapses only under load, the workflow often lacks enough structure to scale
the same mistake across multiple staff points to capability transfer failure, not an isolated person problem
if the same miss keeps traveling through the team, the learning loop is not closing it
coaching without live execution transfer is the cleanest signal of a training gap
without a reinforcement loop, training rarely survives pressured service
visible training transfer failure often expresses itself as standard drift on shift
when resets are discussed but not closed, the learning loop is failing operationally
missing follow-through also signals weak live managerial ownership of the reset
a missing or unavailable live tool is the clearest signal of tooling friction
if the unavailable tool is infrastructure the venue depends on, reliability weakness is also likely
